返回本批次

欧洲人争相购买太阳能、热泵和电动汽车，伊朗战争引发可再生能源热潮

Iran war sparks renewables boom as Europeans rush to buy solar, heat pumps, EVs

www.euronews.com 作者 vrganj 分数 79 评论 97 2026/04/01 22:19
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 随着对伊朗的战争继续凸显化石燃料依赖的普遍风险，绿色能源的理由看起来比以往任何时候都更加充分。自中东冲突爆发以来，全球油价基准布伦特原油价格已飙升逾 50%，在今天（3 月 30 日）早盘交易中触及每桶 116 美元（约合 100.92 欧元）。波动很大程度上归因于霍尔木兹海峡的有效关闭，霍尔木兹海峡是世界上最大的化石燃料阻塞点之一，承载着全球约五分之一的石油供应。每天约有 2000 万桶石油被封锁。欧洲已经感受到了后果，荷兰 TTF 基准天然气价格飙升了 70% 左右，这使得 2026 年 3 月的欧洲天然气价格有望成为自 2021 年 9 月以来最高的月度涨幅。随着能源价格上涨威胁到陷入困境的欧洲人，一些国家已经见证了向绿色技术的显着转变。 “厌倦了受到化石燃料的束缚”英国历来是欧洲使用率最差的国家之一，根据能源公司 Octopus Energy 的数据，3 月前三周的热泵销量比上个月同期增长了 51%。太阳能销量也增长了 54%，因为房主将系统“超大”为 12 个面板，而不是通常的 10 个，而电动汽车 (EV) 充电器销量则攀升了 20%。
  2. “随着人们不再只是提出要求并开始采取行动，我们看到了巨大的转变。英国家庭厌倦了受全球化石燃料价格的束缚，”章鱼能源公司的丽贝卡·迪布-西姆金 (Rebecca Dibb-Simkin) 说。 “通过改用太阳能和热泵，他们正在成为自己的发电站——锁定低成本并长期保护他们的钱包。”加速向电动汽车的转变 欧盟委员会的数据显示，从 2 月 23 日到 3 月 16 日，整个欧盟的平均汽油成本上涨了 12%，达到每升 1.84 欧元。这引发了人们对电动汽车 (EV) 的巨大兴趣，法国在线二手车零售商 Aramisauto 的电动汽车销量在 2 月中旬至 3 月 9 日期间几乎翻了一番。据路透社报道，总部位于阿姆斯特丹的 Olx 表示，法国、罗马尼亚、葡萄牙和波兰市场的电动汽车客户询盘数量激增，“所有市场的增长速度都在持续加速”。在挪威，Finn.no——该国最大的二手车市场——电动汽车实际上已经超过柴油车型，成为该网站最畅销的燃料类型。太阳能转型的德国可再生能源公司 Enpal BV 告诉彭博社，自美以对伊朗战争爆发以来，对太阳能电池板和热泵的询盘增加了约 30%，而太阳能公司 1KOMMA5° GmbH 也报告称，人们对太阳能的兴趣几乎翻了一番。在英国，能源公司 E.ON 发现，2 月 23 日至 3 月 1 日期间，人们对太阳能的兴趣增加了 23%，3 月 2 日至 8 日期间进一步飙升了 63%。 E.ON UK 首席执行官 Chris Norbury 表示：“我们帮助人们控制能源使用并降低账单比以往任何时候都更加重要。” “消费者对太阳能和电池作为解决方案表现出浓厚的兴趣，该产品增加了通过家庭发电和储存能源可以实现的节省。”北海钻探有助于降低能源费用吗？随着绿色技术的蓬勃发展，减少化石燃料使用的呼声越来越高。本月早些时候，英国小报《每日快报》在头版刊登了一篇题为“通过钻探来阻止飙升的账单”的报道，敦促英国开放北海的钻探许可证。然而，牛津大学的一项分析发现，完全由可再生能源供电的英国每年可为家庭节省高达 441 英镑（510 欧元）的能源费用。相比之下，最大限度地提高北海石油和天然气开采量每年只能为家庭节省 16 英镑（19 欧元）至 82 英镑（95 欧元），而这将依赖于分配给家庭的税收收入来抵消其能源费用。该分析报告的合著者阿努帕姆·森博士表示，“排空”北海将使英国能源更加安全并大幅削减家庭开支的想法“纯粹是幻想”。多位专家还指出，石油和天然气价格是由全球市场决定的，对英国消费者来说不打折扣——而且从英国水域提取的天然气可以出口给出价最高的竞标者——这意味着增加国内产量不会显着降低成本。相比之下，西班牙的可再生能源革命一直有助于保持较低的能源费用——即使天然气价格飙升。

Original Summary

  1. The case for green energy looks stronger than ever, as the war on Iran continues to highlight the widespread risks of fossil fuel dependency. Brent crude, the worldwide benchmark for oil prices, has soared more than 50 per cent since the conflict began in the Middle East, hitting $116 (around €100.92) a barrel in early trading today (30 March). Much of the volatility has been attributed to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s biggest fossil fuel chokepoints that carries around one-fifth of global oil supplies. That’s around 20 million barrels being blocked every day. Europe is already feeling the consequences, with the benchmark Dutch TTF natural gas price surging around 70 per cent – putting March 2026 on course to be the highest monthly increase for European gas prices since September 2021. As rising energy prices threaten to hit struggling Europeans, several nations have witnessed a noticeable shift to green technology. ‘Tired of being held hostage by fossil fuels’ The UK, which has historically had one of Europe’s worst uptakes, has seen heat pump sales in the first three weeks of March increase by 51 per cent compared to the same period the month before – according to energy firm Octopus Energy. Solar sales have also increased by 54 per cent, as homeowners “supersize” systems with 12 panels instead of the usual 10, while electric vehicle (EV) charger sales have climbed by 20 per cent.
  2. “We’re seeing a massive shift as people stop just asking and start acting. British families are tired of being held hostage by global fossil fuel prices,” says Rebecca Dibb-Simkin of Octopus Energy. “By switching to solar and heat pumps, they are becoming their own power stations - locking in low costs and protecting their wallets for the long term.” Accelerating the shift to EVs European Commission data shows the average cost of petrol has risen across the EU by 12 per cent to €1.84 per litre from 23 February to 16 March. This has triggered huge interest in Electric Vehicles (EVs), with French online used-car retailer Aramisauto witnessing its EV sales almost double between the middle of February and 9 March. According to Reuters, Amsterdam-based Olx says customer enquiries for EVs have jumped across its marketplaces in France, Romania, Portugal and Poland, with growth “accelerating consistently week-over-week across all markets”. In Norway, Finn.no – the country’s largest used-car marketplace – EVs have actually overtaken diesel models as the site’s best-selling fuel type. A solar-powered transition German renewable energy firm Enpal BV tells Bloomberg that inquiries for solar panels and heat pumps have risen by around 30 per cent since the start of the US-Israel war on Iran, while solar firm 1KOMMA5° GmbH has also reported an almost doubling of interest in solar. In the UK, energy firm E.ON has found interest in solar rose by 23 per cent between 23 February and 1 March, and surged a further 63 per cent between 2 and 8 March. “It’s more important than ever that we help people take control of their energy use and lower their bills,” says Chris Norbury, Chief Executive of E.ON UK. “Consumers are showing strong interest in solar and battery as a solution, and this product adds to the savings that can be achieved by generating and storing energy at home.” Will drilling the North Sea help lower energy bills? Amid the boom in green technology, calls to double down on fossil fuels have gotten louder. Earlier this month, British tabloid the Daily Express printed a frontpage story headlined ‘Get Drilling To Stop Soaring Bills’ – urging the UK to open up drilling licences in the North Sea. However, an analysis from the University of Oxford found that a UK fully powered by renewable energy could save households up to £441 (€510) a year on their energy bills. In comparison, maximising oil and gas extraction from the North Sea would only save households £16 (€19) to £82 (€95) per year – and this would rely on tax revenues being distributed to households to offset their energy bills. Dr Anupam Sen, co-author of the analysis, said the idea that “draining” the North Sea would make the UK more energy secure and significantly cut household bills is “sheer fantasy”. Multiple experts have also pointed out that oil and gas prices are set by global markets, not discounted for British consumers – and gas extracted from UK waters can be exported to the highest bidder – meaning increasing domestic production won’t significantly lower costs. In contrast, Spain’s renewables revolution has been helping to keep energy bills low – even as gas prices soar.

josefritzishere 2026/04/02 00:00
如果美国也这么做就好了。

在 HN 中查看
danans 2026/04/02 00:13
在联邦层面，美国正在倒退。 但在地方和个人层面，很多人第一次意识到他们对全球石油市场的依赖所带来的直接后果，并影响了他们的日常生活。

美国人民仍然不喜欢被当作人质的感觉，这一集清楚地提醒了人们这一点。

20 世纪 70 年代最后一次地缘政治石油冲击导致运输和能源效率大幅提高——这可能会起到同样的作用，但采用的是现有技术。

At a federal level the US is moving backwards. But at a local and personal level, for the first time in generation, a huge number are waking up to the direct consequences of their dependency on the global oil markets and it's impacts their daily lives.

People in the US still don't like feeling like hostages, and this episode is a stark reminder of that.

The last geopolitical oil shocks of the 1970s resulted in huge efficiency increases in transportation and energy - this will likely do the same, but with current technologies.

mothballed 2026/04/02 00:30
在我居住的地方，太阳能发电的难度要高出一万倍。 我能够在没有任何人查看的情况下连接到网格。 横向连接了一个 200 安培的次级连接，直接连接到电网，政府没有任何人眨眼，房子里的电源就打开了。 如果我想要一块 200W 的太阳能电池板，就需要进行代码检查、标记屋顶平面图（我的房子甚至没有建筑平面图，那么如何做到这一点？）、许可证、特殊太阳能债券和特殊保修，然后获得电力公司的许可。

他妈的。

许多县已经做到了这一点，只有当你在一些偏远的地方随意使用太阳能时才有意义，而规划和分区法西斯主义者不会发现你。 在这种情况下，您可以以便宜的数量级安装它，然后它实际上是有意义的。

同时我可以建造一个 200 英尺高的 石油井架 在我的土地上没有任何许可，因为石油公司当然具有政治影响力，可以免除与石油相关的基础设施的要求。

Solar power is 10000x as hard to permit where I live. I was able to connect to the grid without anyone looking at it. Laterally just hooked up a 200amp secondary connection straight to the grid without anyone from the government batting an eye and on the power in the house went. If I wanted even a 200W solar panel it requires a code inspection, a marked roof plan (my house doesn't even have building plans, so how to even do this?), license, special solar bond, and a special warranty and then clearance from the power company.

Fuck that.

Many counties have made it so that solar only makes sense if you are wildcatting it out in some remote place where the planning and zoning fascists won't find you out. In such case you can install it for an order of magnitude cheaper and then it actually makes sense.

Meanwhile I can build a 200 foot tall oil derrick on my land with NO PERMIT WHATSOEVER because of course the oil companies had the political influence to exempt oil related infrastructure from requirements.

toomuchtodo 2026/04/02 00:35
我无法在不知道你住在哪里的情况下谈论你住在哪里，但是 https://www.gosolarapp.org/ 由美国能源部实验室孵化，旨在通过自动化简化住宅许可审批，许多州凌驾于公用事业规模太阳能许可和选址的地方规划之上。

一如既往，这是一个 OSI 第 8 层人员问题；如果你可以并且愿意的话，就参与其中。

I cannot speak to where you live without knowing where you live, but https://www.gosolarapp.org/ was incubated by a DOE lab to streamline residential permitting with automation, and many states override local planning for permitting and siting utility scale solar.

As always, this is an OSI layer 8 people problem; if you can and want to, get involved.

ben_w 2026/04/02 04:40
> 或在北海钻探

我们做到了。那里的大部分石油和天然气现已被开采和出售。石油产量于 1999 年达到顶峰，天然气产量于 2001 年达到顶峰。

如果同一个地方，特别是北海，遍布风力发电场，它可以供应欧盟约一半的电力。

（如果不列颠群岛周围的所有水域都有风电场，那么它就相当于目前欧盟一次能源消耗总量的140%或电力消耗的660%，假设我正确地计算了替代效率乘数）。

猜猜要建造什么？

> Or drilled in the north sea

We did. Most of the oil and gas there has now been removed and sold. Oil production peaked in 1999, gas in 2001.

If the same place, the North Sea specifically, was filled with wind farms, it could supply about half of the EU's electricity.

(If all the waters around the British Isles had wind farms, it becomes 140% of current EU total primary energy consumption or 660% of the electricity consumption, assuming I did the substitution efficiency multiplier right).

Guess what's getting built?

NitpickLawyer 2026/04/02 00:11

是的，不。默克尔关闭核电站以组建联盟的协议100%是个错误。不仅事后看来，由于对俄罗斯天然气的依赖，而且总的来说，这是一个错误。核能以可再生能源无法提供的方式为您提供稳定的能源。我们绝对应该发展更多的可再生能源，但关闭正在运行的核能并不是一件好事。

> better than any of the options you mention.

Yeah, no. Merkel's deal to shut off the nuclear plants to make a coalition was 100% a blunder. Not only in hindsight, with the dependence on russian gas, but in general it was a blunder. Nuclear gives you steady energy in ways that renewables can't. We should absolutely do more renewables, but to shut off working nuclear was not good.

在 HN 中查看
fpoling 2026/04/02 00:28
核电并没有那么稳定。核电站需要大量的水来冷却。当一个特别炎热的夏季到来时，河流会干涸，核反应堆必须缩小发电规模，甚至关闭。然后它们需要定期进行大量维护。

诚然，在欧洲炎热干燥的夏季正是太阳能发电高峰期。因此，在核能最理想的情况下，这比寒冷的冬天、多云、无风的问题要小得多。

仍然从 20 年前的角度来看，可再生能源前景不明，天然气发电站被认为比核能更可靠、更灵活，也比煤炭更清洁。当然，只要有人得到汽油。

Nuclear is not that steady. Nuclear plants require a lot of water to cool things. And when a particular hot summer happens, rivers dry out and nuclear reactors have to scale down the power production or even be shutdown. And then they require quite significant maintenance periodically.

Granted, in Europe a hot dry summer is when solar is at its peak. So it is much lesser problem than a cold winter with a lot of cloudy days with no wind when nuclear energy is ideal.

Still from a perspective of 20 years ago with unknown prospects about renewables natural gas power stations were considered much more reliable and flexible power source compared with nuclear and way more cleaner than coal. Of cause, as long as one gets gas.

this_user 2026/04/02 00:35
说是默克尔决定关闭核电站的说法完全是错误的。这个决定是十多年前做出的。在之前曾尝试部分取消计划但未成功后，她最终只是加快了计划的进度。这甚至不是她的决定，而是福岛事故后人们强烈反对核能的意愿，她只是执行了它。

ben_w 2026/04/02 05:03
规划的图表从 2005 年到 2050 年几乎是一条直线，它非常接近德国最好的刻板印象。

大约十年前，这被描述为：

  while the German approach is not unique worldwide, the speed and scope of the Energiewende are exceptional
虽然他们本可以在一个神奇的平行宇宙中做得更好，那里的人们并不害怕核能，但事实上这种转变非常快。

有关显示核能与可再生能源规模的更详细图表，包括您特别批评的时期，请参阅第 12 页： https://web.archive.org/web/20160602074457/https://www.agora...

The planned graph is an almost straight line from 2005 to 2050, which it is following very closely in the best of German stereotypes.

A decade or so ago, this was described as:

  while the German approach is not unique worldwide, the speed and scope of the Energiewende are exceptional
While they could've done better in a magical alternate universe where the population was not terrified of nuclear power, the transition has in fact been very fast.

For a more detailed graph showing the scale of nuclear vs. renewable, including the period you're criticising in particular, page 12: https://web.archive.org/web/20160602074457/https://www.agora...

我也不同意美国人更自由或更富有的说法。 伊朗的冒险，即使立即结束，也已经让另一代美国人失去了公费医疗的机会，让典型的美国人比工资显示的要贫穷得多。 地面入侵很容易让美国破产。与此同时，特朗普正试图以大宪章前的国王身份行事，而负责阻止他的法院在压力下迅速崩溃。 这是专制主义的严重倒退。

I also take issue with the claim that Americans are freer or richer. The Iranian adventure, even were it to end immediately, has taken socialized medicine off the table for another generation of Americans, leaving typical Americans a lot poorer than salaries suggest. This is a serious backslide into authoritarianism.

tharmas 2026/04/02 00:38
特朗普的讽刺之处在于，他有时会把安静的部分大声说出来。他是一个病态的骗子，但同时他又说真话。他透露了美国统治精英想让加拿大成为附庸国的愿望。可以说，当布赖恩·莫罗尼（Brian Mulroney）（他本人最初反对，但商业游说团体告诉他不然：他尽职尽责地遵守他的捐助者）推动并签署自由贸易协定时，加拿大精英做到了这一点：“我正在掷骰子！”。他被说服首先通过选举做出决定。他赢得了多数席位，但没有赢得普选。无论如何，他还是签了字。现在，加拿大发现自己处于他的反对派警告的境地：把鸡蛋放在一个篮子里是冒着美国不会被法西斯独裁者统治的巨大风险。

但多亏了法西斯独裁者，加拿大人再次醒悟，认识到将自己与一个流氓巨人如此紧密地联系在一起是愚蠢的。共和党应该为自己向特朗普磕头而深感羞愧。请注意，共和党有很多事情应该感到羞耻——他们通过诱导贫困的政策，帮助创造了使特朗普当选成为可能的局面。共和党和纳粹党一样令人厌恶。

然后是无能的民主党人。绝对没用。

The irony about tRump is he sometimes says the quiet part out loud. He is a pathological liar yet at the same time he speaks truth. He revealed the USA's ruling Elite's desire to make Canada a vassal state. Arguably, the Canadian Elite did it when Brian Mulroney, (he was originally against it himself but the Business lobby told him otherwise: he dutifully complied with his donors), pushed and signed the free trade agreement: "I'm rolling the dice!". He was persuaded to put the decision to an election first. He won the majority of seats, but not the popular vote. He signed it anyway. Now, Canada finds itself in the position that his opposition warned about: that putting your eggs in one basket was taking a big risk that US wasn't going to be ruled by a Fascist Dictator.

But thanks to the Fascist Dictator Canadians have once again woken up to the folly of tying yourself so closely to a giant who goes rogue. The Republican Party should be deeply ashamed of themselves for kowtowing to tRump. Mind you, there is plenty of things the Republican Party should be ashamed about - they helped create the situation that would make the election of tRump possible - with their poverty inducing policies. The Republican Party is as loathsome as the Nazi Party.

And then there is the feckless Democrats. Absolutely useless.

guywithahat 2026/04/02 01:04
更进一步说，大规模移民进入欧洲并不是最近才发生的事情，而是最近才发生的事情。这种事已经发生了几十年了。有一段时间，伊斯兰国鼓励在欧洲发动袭击，圣战分子在圣诞节游行和类似活动中驾驶汽车，造成数百人死亡，这种活动在 2016 年和 2017 年达到顶峰。我认为最大的一次袭击是巴士底日在法国尼斯发生的袭击，造成 86 人死亡、数百人受伤（https://grokipedia.com/page/2016_Nice_truck_attack）我能想到的另一起著名事件是柏林圣诞市场袭击事件，造成 12 人死亡、56 人受伤（https://grokipedia.com/page/2016_Berlin_truck_attack）。这些是经济移民的结果，与美国所做的任何具体事情无关。
And to further your point mass immigration into Europe isn't just recent; it's been happening for decades. For a while the Islamic state was encouraging attacks in Europe, and hundreds of people were killed by jihadists running cars through Christmas parades and similar events which peaked ~2016 and 2017. I think the largest was an attack in Nice, France on Bastille day killing 86 and injuring hundreds (https://grokipedia.com/page/2016_Nice_truck_attack) and another famous one I can think of was the christmas market attack in Berlin, killing 12 and injuring 56 (https://grokipedia.com/page/2016_Berlin_truck_attack). These were the result of economic immigration, unrelated to anything specific the US had done.

ben_w 2026/04/02 04:54
石油（接近）是可替代的，这意味着美国燃油泵的较高价格对德黑兰和莫斯科独裁者的经济支持与欧盟燃油泵一样多。

具有讽刺意味的是，“接近”部分足以阻止美国通过提炼和使用目前出口的产品来将自己与世界市场隔离。

Oil is (close to) fungible, which means the higher prices in US fuel pumps are just as much financially supporting dictators in Tehran and Moscow as EU fuel pumps.

Ironically, the "close to" part is just enough to prevent the USA from isolating itself from the world market by refining and using what it currently exports.

storus 2026/04/02 00:30
电动汽车的续航里程仍然有点令人印象深刻——理想情况下，450英里/700公里或5分钟20->80%的充电，以可接受的价格（3.5万欧元）应该是常态。对于城市来说这并不重要，但对于更长的假期旅行来说这是必须的，没有人愿意在1100公里的行程上浪费3个小时充电。中国电动汽车可能能够以这个价格点交付（比亚迪），但欧盟额外增加（高达）45％的额外费用，以惩罚中国电动汽车制造商并防止欧盟汽车制造商倒闭。
EVs are still a bit underwhelming wrt range - ideally either 450miles/700km or 5 minute 20->80% recharge at an acceptable price (35k EUR) should be the norm. For cities it doesn't matter but for longer vacation trips it's a must, nobody wants to waste 3 hours on a 1100km trip recharging. Chinese EVs might be able to deliver it at this price point (BYD) but EU adds additional (up to) 45% in extra fees to penalize Chinese EV makers and to prevent collapse of EU car makers.

toomuchtodo 2026/04/02 00:40
去年全球销售的汽车中有四分之一是电动汽车，占全球最大市场中国月销量的 50% 以上。尽管电动汽车将继续快速改善续航里程和充电基础设施，但大部分已得到解决。挪威的电动汽车月销量约为 100%，其他国家最终也会达到这一水平。

重要的是，随着电动汽车销售达到内燃机销售已下降到无法再支持内燃机汽车制造商持续经营的水平，我们应该预期会更快。全球内燃汽车销量峰值出现在 2017 年。

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47459145 （引文）

1 in 4 vehicles sold globally last year were EVs, and they are >50% of the monthly sales in China, the largest market in the world. EVs are mostly solved, even though they will continue to rapidly improve, both range and charging infrastructure. Norway is at ~100% monthly EV sales, other countries will get there eventually.

Importantly, we should expect to go faster as EV sales reach a point where combustion sales have declined to a level where they can no longer support combustion vehicle manufacturers as a going concern. Peak global combustion auto sales occurred in 2017.

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47459145 (citations)

在 HN 中查看
storus 2026/04/02 00:50
趋势很明显，但由于我上面提到的原因，目前他们无法取代内燃机汽车。它们要么缺乏续航里程/充电便利性，要么没有但价格太贵。他们还需要几年的时间来扩大规模，或者欧盟需要停止惩罚中国电动汽车。
The trend is clear but right now they aren't able to replace ICE cars due to what I mentioned above. Either they lack range/recharging convenience or they don't but are too expensive. They need a few more years of scaling or EU to stop penalizing Chinese EVs.

DoctorOetker 2026/04/02 07:46
我不明白国际社会和美国如何处理伊朗/霍尔木兹海峡危机。

从伊朗一再宣布正在做出这样那样的让步，然后其他各方一再否认，可能会发生多种情况：

1）也许白宫正在编造/天真的他们在通讯线路的另一端有谁，声称代表伊朗

2）也许伊朗故意做出不真诚的承诺，只是为了通过次要渠道否认来羞辱美国

3）也许伊朗实际上存在不同的派别，而且根本不清楚谁将组建（新的？）政府。

观看此节目不符合任何人的兴趣。

假设伊朗领导人（政客、官员……）可以生成自己的加密密钥，并通过签署他们亲自会面并确认密钥的声明来承认彼此的密钥，是否合理？

到那时，国际社会可以建立一个网站，不同派系可以在其中注册不同的声称的内部联盟，并证明他们对特定联盟的支持，但有一个限制：在任何时候，一个人只能支持单一的职能分配建议（谁坐在哪里），也许还有第二、第三等第​​N种偏好。

当一个政权正在崩溃时，最大签署提案会经常变化，直到新政权的代表稳定下来。

假设局势不稳定：伊朗不能公开宣称他们稳定了 政治上有弹性 反对美国/以色列的袭击（无论其正义如何）。

假设它确实稳定下来：到那时我们已经确定了谁在当地掌权（再次无论正义如何：也许一些加密签名是通过用枪指着头部做出的；但这不会改变结果的准确性：谁掌权？）

一旦局势稳定下来，并且当前谁掌权一目了然，那么国际社会就可以简单地观察到伊朗遵守与自身不矛盾的加密签名主张（提高其在谈判中的可信度），或者观察到伊朗在与美国沟通时以加密方式签署一件事，而在与其他方沟通时以完全不同的方式签署另一件事，从而损害其自身的可信度。

I don't understand how both the international community as well as say US is dealing with the Iran / Straight of Hormuz crisis.

From the repeated announcements that Iran is making such and such concessions and then repeat denial through other parties multiple things could be going on:

1) perhaps the White House is making up / being naive about who they have on the other end of a communication line, claiming to represent Iran

2) perhaps Iran is intentionally providing insincere promises only to humiliate the US through secondary channels by denial

3) perhaps there are in fact different factions in Iran and its simply not clear who gets to form the (new?) government.

Watching this scatshow is in Nobodies interest.

Is it reasonable to assume that leaders in Iran (politicians, functionaries, ...) can generate their own cryptographic keys, and acknowledge each others keys by signing claims that they met and confirmed the keys in person?

At that point the international community can put up a website where different factions can sign up to different claimed internal coalitions, and prove their support for a specific coalition, with one constraint: at all times one can only support a single functionary allocation proposal (who sits where), and perhaps 2nd, 3rd, etc N'th preferences.

While a regime is experiencing collapse the maximum signatory proposal will change frequently, until stabilizing to the new regime representatives.

Assume it doesnt stabilize: Iran can't openly claim they are politically resilient against the US/Israeli strikes (regardless of their justice).

Assume it does stabilize: at that point we have identified who is locally in power (again regardless of justice: perhaps some of the cryptographic signatures were made by pointing guns at heads; but that doesn't change the veracity of the result: who is in power?)

Once it has stabilized, and it is clear who is currently in power, then the international community can simply either observe an Iran compliant with cryptographically signed claims not contradicting itself (improving its credibility in negotiations), or observe an Iran that cryptographically signs one thing when communicating with US and a totally different thing when communicating through other parties, damaging its own credibility.

DoctorOetker 2026/04/02 09:24
看来这条消息飞过你的头顶

这不是关于你或我的信仰的民意调查，它指出至少存在一种比所追求的噪音更低的途径

请注意，您的位置无法被全世界验证，而加密签名的消息将被全世界的我们个人验证。

有人可能会反驳说，美国可能会编造大量假加密密钥/伊朗协会；但可以肯定的是，只要伊朗有一个运转良好的政权，它肯定可以利用国际原子能机构作为传达伊朗密码关键观察结果的渠道，例如：

* 哈梅内伊确认亲自会见阿拉格奇，并且阿拉格奇选择了这样那样的公钥

* 阿拉格奇确认亲自会见哈梅内伊，哈梅内伊选择了这样那样的公钥

*等等，适合所有有兴趣参与确认现有政权或在混乱中建立新政权的玩家。

（如果你无法控制核电站的物理学家，那么你就无法控制你的政权）

it seems the message flew over your head

this isn't a poll about what you or I believe, it's pointing to the existence of at least one lower-noise avenue than the ones pursued

observe that your position is not verifiable by the world at large, while cryptographically signed messages would be veriable by us individuals across the whole world.

one may counter that the US could make up a large number of fake crypto key / Iranian associations; but surely to the extent that Iran has a functioning regime, surely it could use the IAEA as a channel to communicate the Iranian cryptographic key observations like:

* Khamenei confirms meeting Aragchi in person and Aragchi has chosen such and such a public key

* Aragchi confirms meeting Khamenei in person and Khamenei has chosen such and such a public key

* and so on for all players interested in participating in confirming the present regime or establishing a new one amidst chaos.

(if you don't have control over your physicists at a nuclear power plant, then you don't have control over your regime)

在 HN 中查看
toomuchtodo 2026/04/02 00:11
> 这听起来极其危险；如果没有电工完成并经过城市检查，您就无法在美国添加光伏（或任何辅助电源）并将其连接到公用电源。

这实际上是不准确的。犹他州是第一个将插电式太阳能合法化的州，其他 17 个州也正在立法中。听起来您不了解欧洲和美国有关离岛的规定。这是一个已解决的问题。

> 最大的监管问题——停电期间为线路通电并使线路工人面临危险——实际上并不是一个问题，因为逆变器受 UL 1741 的保护，并且具有“反孤岛”能力。

UL 3700 专门解决并减轻太阳能插入风险。

有关以下主题的资源：

https://pv-magazine-usa.com/2026/03/27/the-theory-and-practi...

https://solarunitedneighbors.org/resources/what-to-know-abou...

https://www.ul.com/news/ul-solutions-debuts-testing-and-cert...

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NANIjW3yGsFhpNTzvg30kMHo...

https://permitpower.org/wp-content/uploads/sites/35/2026/01/...

https://www.cesa.org/resource-library/resource/plug-in-solar...

https://www.canarymedia.com/articles/solar/balcony-solar-tak...

https://www.brightsaver.org/publicly-filed-states

https://www.digikey.com/en/articles/anti-islanding-and-smart...

> That sounds extremely dangerous; you cannot add PV (or any secondary source) in the US and connect it to utility power without it being done by an electrician and inspected by the city.

This is factually inaccurate. Utah was the first to legalize plug in solar, and 17 other states have legislation pending to do so. It sounds like you are unaware of regulations around islanding in both Europe and the US. This is a solved problem.

> The biggest regulatory concern – energizing lines during an outage and putting line workers at risk – is not really an issue, since inverters are covered by UL 1741, and have “anti-islanding” capability.

UL 3700 specifically addresses plug in solar risks and mitigating them.

Resources on the topic below:

https://pv-magazine-usa.com/2026/03/27/the-theory-and-practi...
